popular_liberalism_4T: The other (the 'oligarchy' and its instruments) is judged morally bad, not merely different
procedural_liberalism: The other (the popular majority) is judged not bad but epistemically deficient, to be guided

popular_liberalism_4T: Assimilation: the people folded into one moral-national subject; dissenters redescribed as outside the nation
procedural_liberalism: Containment: the majority bounded by counter-majoritarian institutions and procedure

popular_liberalism_4T: Claims intimate knowledge of 'the people' while refusing to know its internal plurality
procedural_liberalism: Characteristic failure: refuses to know the popular subject on its own terms

popular_liberalism_4T: Moral-national belonging: those faithful to the popular project
procedural_liberalism: Rights-bearing citizenship: equal individuals under law

popular_liberalism_4T: Moral exclusion: dissent recoded as betrayal / antinational
procedural_liberalism: Procedural exclusion: the majority distrusted, distanced from direct rule
